title: Dashing Australian Kangaroo Curry
description: A fatty, snappy, energizing Australian dish featuring massaman paste and namul‑blanch seasoning, free of onion and garlic.

ingredients.name: namul blanch seasoning
ingredients.notes: toasted sesame oil, toasted sesame seeds, seaweed flakes, chili flakes

instructions: Slice kangaroo steak into bite‑sized cubes.
Marinate the cubes in 1 tbsp of namul blanch seasoning and 1 tsp salt for 10 minutes.
Heat a wok over medium‑high heat and add 1 tbsp coconut oil; sear kangaroo cubes until browned on all sides, then remove and set aside.
In the same wok, add diced carrot, sliced bell pepper, and trimmed green beans; stir‑fry for 3 minutes until just tender.
Stir in massaman curry paste and cook for 1 minute to release aromas.
Return the kangaroo cubes to the wok, pour in coconut milk, soy sauce, and lime juice; bring to a gentle simmer.
Simmer uncovered for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce thickens and the kangaroo is cooked through.
Taste and adjust seasoning with additional salt if needed.
Remove from heat, transfer to a serving dish, and garnish with chopped fresh coriander.
Serve hot with steamed rice or flatbread.
